most_recent_game.recap_text:
DAVIDSON — Fordham scored 15 runs in the ninth inning and rolled to a 26-3 series-clinching win at Wilson Field Sunday afternoon.
With the loss, the Wildcats dropped to 9-9 overall and 1-2 in the Atlantic 10.
Fordham (8-10, 2-1) led 2-0 early before
Theo Loughlin
tied the game with a two-run single in the fourth. Then the Rams scored six in the fifth and three in the seventh to break open the game. But they did most of their damage in the ninth off nine hits, including a Madden Ocko grand slam, and two Wildcat errors.
Will Brooks
provided the other Wildcat offensive highlight, an RBI-double in the seventh.
Starter
Ed Hall
threw 4.2 innings in the loss.
Up Next
The Wildcats are idle in the midweek and will play at A-10 foe La Salle next weekend.

f8e3d382.name: Justin Higgins
f8e3d382.narrative: Saw limited action in a blowout loss to Fordham, recording a strikeout in his only at-bat as the Wildcats surrendered 15 runs in the ninth inning during a 26-3 defeat. Early in his sophomore campaign with just one game played, Higgins remains winless on the season with zero hits and will look to contribute as Davidson regroups for Atlantic 10 conference play against La Salle.

edf25899.name: Fredy Diaz
edf25899.narrative: Diaz went hitless in three at-bats as Davidson's offense crumbled in a 26-3 blowout loss to Fordham, with the Rams exploding for 15 runs in the ninth inning to clinch the series. Through 14 games, the sophomore infielder is hitting .230 with just nine hits and no home runs, struggling to establish consistent production early in the season.

3de2c103.name: Jacob Vokal
3de2c103.narrative: Vokal entered in relief during Davidson's fifth consecutive victory over Fordham, surrendering two runs on two hits across 1.2 innings in the Atlantic 10 Conference opener. Through 13.0 IP this season, the dual-position prospect has posted a 9.00 ERA with 6 K, signaling continued development needed in his early collegiate pitching role.

26604fb2.name: Matthew Vannoy
26604fb2.narrative: In a lopsided 26-3 loss to Fordham, Vannoy drew two walks despite limited offensive opportunities as the Wildcats' lineup was overwhelmed by a devastating ninth-inning collapse. The freshman backstop/infielder has maintained a solid .328 average through 18 games, flashing plate discipline with multiple walks in a season where he's been among Davidson's more consistent contact hitters early in the year.

2ac1ffba.name: Aidan Rice
2ac1ffba.narrative: Rice contributed a crucial RBI single in the fourth inning as Davidson extended its lead to five runs en route to a 9-8 comeback victory over Fordham, the Wildcats' fifth consecutive win to open conference play. Through 13 games, the senior infielder/outfielder has maintained a .343 average with 5 RBIs despite limited power production, establishing himself as a consistent contact bat in Davidson's lineup.

fb3108b4.name: Gavin Thomas
fb3108b4.narrative: Limited offensive opportunities marked Thomas' afternoon in Davidson's 6-4 loss to Fordham, as he managed one RBI despite recording just one at-bat in a contest where the Wildcats' potent offense was largely silenced by Rams starter Chase Hamawalt's 13-strikeout performance. Through 13 games, Thomas has yet to establish consistency at the plate (.222 AVG, 4 H), though his two home runs suggest raw power potential that needs regular playing time to develop.

e710cc73.name: Cooper Collins
e710cc73.narrative: In a blowout loss where Fordham's ninth-inning explosion overshadowed individual performances, Collins went hitless in limited action as Davidson's offense struggled to keep pace in the 26-3 defeat. Still searching for his first hit of the season through three games, the sophomore infielder will look to contribute when the Wildcats host La Salle next weekend in Atlantic 10 play.

adae7002.name: Will McCullough
adae7002.narrative: Went hitless in limited action as Davidson's offense was overwhelmed in a 26-3 blowout loss to Fordham, with the Rams erupting for 15 ninth-inning runs to clinch the series. Through nine games, McCullough remains stuck at a .173 average with just four hits and no extra-base production, indicating early struggles in his freshman campaign that require immediate adjustments.

566d5c4f.name: Matt Manjooran
566d5c4f.narrative: Went hitless in a blowout loss where Fordham erupted for 15 ninth-inning runs, though drew a walk in three plate appearances as Davidson's offense was overwhelmed 26-3. Through 18 games as a freshman, Manjooran has established himself as a contact hitter (.338 AVG, 23 H) with gap power potential (1 HR, 20 RBI) while splitting time between third base and the outfield.

bb536822.name: Jamie Daly
bb536822.narrative: Daly went hitless with three strikeouts in a lopsided 26-3 loss to Fordham, continuing to struggle at the plate as Davidson's offense was overwhelmed by a ninth-inning barrage. The sophomore's 0-for-4 performance marks a significant dip from his .404 season average through 16 games, raising questions about consistency against quality Atlantic 10 competition.

c1a018d8.name: Forrest Lietz
c1a018d8.narrative: In a lopsided 26-3 loss to Fordham, Lietz managed a hit in four at-bats as the Wildcats surrendered a devastating nine-run ninth inning that buried any offensive momentum. Through 17 games, the junior outfielder/left-hander has emerged as Davidson's most consistent bat at .435 with 27 hits and a team-leading 17 RBIs, though the team sits 1-2 in Atlantic 10 play with work ahead.

ac58f0bf.name: Brycen Champey
ac58f0bf.narrative: Entered in relief during Fordham's ninth-inning explosion (15 runs on nine hits) and allowed three earned runs over 1.1 IP with a 2:2 K:BB ratio in the 26-3 loss. Through 11.0 early-season innings, Champey has posted a concerning 8.18 ERA despite solid strikeout rate (14 K), suggesting command and run prevention remain areas requiring development as a senior contributor in the Davidson rotation.

11cd9e28.name: Keely Furr
11cd9e28.narrative: In a narrow 11-10 victory over Gardner-Webb, Furr recorded a brief appearance of 0.2 innings with 4 walks and 2 strikeouts, contributing to Davidson's fourth straight win and improving the Wildcats to 8-7. Through 5.2 IP on the season, the junior righty has logged 7 strikeouts against a concerning 25.96 ERA, signaling continued development needs as a depth piece in the relief corps.

a7f138c0.name: Theo Loughlin
a7f138c0.narrative: In a blowout loss to Fordham, Loughlin provided Davidson's lone bright spot with a two-run single in the fourth that briefly tied the game before the Rams' offense exploded late. The freshman first baseman sits at .327 through 17 games this season with 13 RBI and no home runs, establishing himself as a consistent contact bat for the Wildcats' lineup.

716a2b6a.name: Cooper Cavanaugh
716a2b6a.narrative: Cavanaugh lasted just 3.1 innings against Fordham on Saturday, unable to build on Davidson's five-game win streak as the Rams' dominant starter Chase Hamawalt set the tone early with 13 strikeouts through six innings. Through 12.1 IP this season, the junior lefty sits at a 6.69 ERA with control issues (9 walks in limited work) that will need tightening as conference play intensifies.

993a289b.name: Scotty Edwards
993a289b.narrative: In a lopsided 26-3 loss to Fordham, Edwards recorded one hit in three at-bats as the Wildcats' offense was overwhelmed by a nine-run ninth-inning onslaught. Through eight games, the senior outfielder maintained a solid .370 average with 2 home runs, though he'll look to build on modest RBI production (8) as Davidson regroups for Atlantic 10 play.

1b623240.name: Will Banks
1b623240.narrative: Entered in a blowout situation against Fordham, Banks recorded just one of 27 outs before allowing two earned runs on three hits in a historically lopsided 26-3 defeat. The senior right-hander's early-season struggles—180.00 ERA across 0.2 innings—signal Davidson will need significant contributions from other arms in its Atlantic 10 rotation.

b58826ad.name: Ed Hall
b58826ad.narrative: Charged with five earned runs across 4.2 innings against Fordham, Hall couldn't escape the fifth inning as the Rams' offense mounted early before exploding for 15 runs in a ninth-inning collapse that defined Davidson's 26-3 series loss. Through 19.2 season innings, the senior righty's 6.56 ERA and 8 earned runs allowed reflect early struggles that could limit his role down the stretch in Atlantic 10 play.

864fced3.name: Anthony Torreso
864fced3.narrative: Went 2-for-3 with a walk in a blowout loss to Fordham, continuing to reach base consistently despite the Wildcats' ninth-inning collapse that saw the Rams score 15 unanswered runs. Through 18 games, Torreso has posted a .268 average with 11 RBIs but remains without a home run, suggesting a contact-oriented profile that will need to demonstrate more power potential as the season progresses.

ffcbd0bc.name: Will Brooks
ffcbd0bc.narrative: Brooks logged an RBI-double in the seventh inning of Sunday's lopsided loss to Fordham, providing one of Davidson's few offensive bright spots in a 26-3 defeat that saw the Rams explode for 15 ninth-inning runs. Through 13 games, the sophomore utility player owns a .261 average with 7 RBI, demonstrating modest offensive production while competing for playing time at a skill position.

f0c5a8df.name: Ivan Hoyt
f0c5a8df.narrative: Hoyt notched his second save in three days by striking out two in a scoreless ninth inning Friday, helping Davidson secure its fifth consecutive win and Atlantic 10 Conference opener against Fordham. The right-hander has posted a 2.53 ERA over his last four appearances (7.1 IP, 11 K) after an early-season rough patch, positioning himself as a reliable late-inning option despite his season 5.29 mark across 10.2 innings.

a7bd1753.name: Brendan Fritch
a7bd1753.narrative: Held hitless in two at-bats against Fordham's dominant starter Chase Hamawalt, who struck out 13 in six innings, Fritch remained silent as Davidson's five-game win streak ended in a 6-4 loss. Through 13 games as a freshman, the C/OF has struggled to find consistent contact at .195 with just nine hits and no power production, signaling an adjustment period at the college level.

2020d833.name: Will Papciak
2020d833.narrative: Struggled in a mop-up role against Wake Forest, surrendering 2 runs on 1 hit while walking 4 in just 1.0 IP, continuing a rough early-season stretch. Through 2.0 innings pitched this season, Papciak has posted an 18.00 ERA with 4 earned runs allowed against just 2 strikeouts, signaling significant mechanical or command issues that need immediate attention.

5d1a54a5.name: Tyler Wille
5d1a54a5.narrative: Wille saw minimal action in a blowout loss to Fordham, entering briefly in a ninth-inning collapse where the Rams plated 15 runs on nine hits and two Davidson errors to seal a 26-3 rout. Through two appearances this season, the sophomore left-hander has struggled early—posting a 90.00 ERA across 0.2 innings with two earned runs allowed, leaving room for bounce-back opportunities as Davidson enters conference play.

f3222aea.name: Daniel Smith
f3222aea.narrative: Called in during a ninth-inning collapse where Fordham plundered 15 runs on nine hits, Smith recorded just one out before exiting with three hits and four earned runs allowed in a 26-3 series loss. The outing epitomizes a brutal start to his sophomore season: 3.1 innings pitched across multiple appearances, 9 earned runs, and a 26.13 ERA that signals significant command and stuff concerns requiring immediate attention.

5ae35ed1.name: Parker Jones
5ae35ed1.narrative: Entered a blowout situation in relief against Fordham, recording a clean 1.1 innings with a strikeout in a loss that snapped Davidson's five-game win streak. Early-season relief role showing promise (2.81 ERA, 2 K in 3.2 IP) as a potential depth piece in the Wildcats' rotation.

54fe2e8a.name: Will Marenghi
54fe2e8a.narrative: Marenghi entered in relief against Fordham's 13-strikeout starter Chase Hamawalt, recording two strikeouts across two innings while the Wildcats' five-game win streak fell to a 6-4 defeat. Through 12.2 innings this season, the sophomore right-hander sits at a 4.43 ERA with modest strikeout production, suggesting room for refinement in command and swing-and-miss rates as Davidson navigates early conference play.

b49fd101.name: Garrett Pyne
b49fd101.narrative: Pyne entered in relief during Fordham's explosive ninth-inning outburst that saw the Rams plate 15 runs en route to a 26-3 victory, inheriting a game already well out of reach. Through 8.0 early-season innings, the freshman righty has surrendered 8 earned runs (9.00 ERA, 5 K) while establishing himself in Davidson's relief mix.

a7ad9a02.name: Carson Taggart
a7ad9a02.narrative: Taggart saw minimal work in Davidson's fifth straight win, entering briefly in a high-leverage spot against Fordham but struggling to record an out while surrendering three hits and four runs in just 0.1 innings. The rough outing underscores early-season inconsistency for the freshman southpaw, who owns a 6.85 ERA through 9.2 frames with eight strikeouts—a sign that command and durability remain works in progress at the college level.

c87dda45.name: Jed Howard
c87dda45.narrative: Went hitless in limited action as Davidson's five-game winning streak fell to Fordham's dominant pitching display, with Hamawalt's 13 strikeouts leaving the Wildcats with just one hit through six innings. Early season struggles persist at 0-for-4 with a strikeout across four games, warranting close monitoring of his approach and role definition moving forward.

71b9a565.name: Wilson Perkins
71b9a565.narrative: Perkins settled in effectively through six innings to notch a quality start in Davidson's fifth consecutive victory, allowing just four hits and three walks while striking out four despite early Fordham pressure. The right-hander's 6.49 ERA masks improved command in recent outings, though a 16 ER in 22.2 IP overall signals he'll need sustained consistency to remain a reliable rotation piece down the stretch.

85831c5c.name: Simon Yochum
85831c5c.narrative: Yochum entered in relief during Davidson's fifth-game winning streak finale against Fordham, surrendering three runs on four hits across 2.1 innings in the Wildcats' 6-4 loss. Through 13.1 IP this season, the sophomore has struck out 18 but carries a bloated 9.62 ERA, suggesting command and consistency issues that will need addressing for meaningful weekend role opportunities in A-10 play.
